# WAVS Foundry Template

> A template project for building Autonomous Verifiable Services (AVS) using WAVS (WASI-AVS runtime). Enables creation, deployment, and operation of AVS components with on-chain verifiability through WASM/WASI execution and EigenLayer security.

WAVS enables building decentralized services that execute off-chain computation with on-chain verifiability, leveraging WASM/WASI for near-native performance and EigenLayer for security. The platform supports dynamic deployment and updates through a composable service architecture.

## Core Documentation
Please read through each of the linked files for a basic understanding of WAVS.
### Main Documentation
- [Overview](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/overview.mdx): Core concepts, use cases, and basic service architecture
- [How It Works](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/how-it-works.mdx): Technical architecture, execution flow, and WASM/WASI runtime details
- [Design Considerations](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/design.mdx): Best practices, deterministic execution guidelines, and state management
- [Benefits](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/benefits.mdx): Platform advantages and features

### Tutorial: Building a Prediction Market Oracle
Follow this step-by-step guide to build a prediction market oracle using WAVS. For a complete list of setup and execution commands, see the [repository README](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/README.md):
- [1. Overview](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/tutorial/1-overview.mdx): Introduction and architecture overview
- [2. Setup](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/tutorial/2-setup.mdx): Environment setup and prerequisites
- [3. Project](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/tutorial/3-project.mdx): Project structure and configuration
- [4. Component](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/tutorial/4-component.mdx): Building the WASM component
- [5. Build](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/tutorial/5-build.mdx): Building and testing the service
- [6. Run Service](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/tutorial/6-run-service.mdx): Deploying and running the service
- [7. Prediction](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docs/tutorial/7-prediction.mdx): Using the prediction market oracle

## Template Structure

### Configuration Files
- [`.env.example`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/.env.example): Environment configuration template
  ```env
  # Core WAVS settings
  WAVS_DATA=~/wavs/data
  WAVS_LOG_LEVEL="info"
  WAVS_SUBMISSION_MNEMONIC="test test test test test test test test test test test junk"
  
  # CLI and Aggregator settings
  WAVS_CLI_DATA=~/wavs/cli
  WAVS_AGGREGATOR_DATA=~/wavs/aggregator
  ```
  Environment variables control WAVS node behavior, CLI operations, and aggregator settings. Custom env vars can be added for component-specific needs.

- [`Makefile`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/Makefile): Build and deployment automation
  ```makefile
  # Customize these variables for your service
  COMPONENT_FILENAME=eth_price_oracle.wasm
  TRIGGER_EVENT="NewTrigger(bytes)"
  SERVICE_CONFIG='{"fuel_limit":100000000,"max_gas":5000000,"host_envs":[],"kv":[],"workflow_id":"default","component_id":"default"}'
  ```
  The Makefile provides commands for building components (`make wasi-build`), deploying services (`make deploy-service`), and managing the development environment.

### Smart Contracts
- [`src/contracts/WavsSubmit.sol`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/src/contracts/WavsSubmit.sol): Service handler implementation
  ```solidity
  contract SimpleSubmit is ISimpleSubmit, IWavsServiceHandler {
      mapping(TriggerId => bool) internal _validTriggers;
      mapping(TriggerId => bytes) internal _datas;
      mapping(TriggerId => bytes) internal _signatures;
      
      function handleSignedData(bytes calldata _data, bytes calldata _signature) external {
          _serviceManager.validate(_data, _signature);
          // Store validated data and signature
      }
  }
  ```
  The service handler validates and processes signed data from WAVS operators. It implements `IWavsServiceHandler` for integration with the WAVS platform.

### WASM Components
- [`components/eth-price-oracle/src/lib.rs`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/components/eth-price-oracle/src/lib.rs): Example price oracle implementation
  ```rust
  impl Guest for Component {
      fn run(action: TriggerAction) -> Result<Option<Vec<u8>>, String> {
          // Decode trigger event data
          let (trigger_id, req, dest) = decode_trigger_event(action.data)?;
          
          // Parse input and fetch price data
          let id = input.chars().next().ok_or("Empty input")?.to_digit(16)? as u64;
          let res = block_on(async move { get_price_feed(id).await })?;
          
          // Encode response based on destination
          Ok(match dest {
              Destination::Ethereum => Some(encode_trigger_output(trigger_id, &res)),
              Destination::CliOutput => Some(res),
          })
      }
  }
  ```
  Components are compiled to WASM and run in the WAVS runtime. They implement the `Guest` trait to handle trigger events and return results.

## Development Workflow

### 1. Environment Setup
- Copy [`.env.example`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/.env.example) to `.env`
- Configure environment variables:
  ```bash
  # Copy environment template
  cp .env.example .env
  
  # Install dependencies and setup project
  make setup
  ```

### 2. Component Development
- Create new component in [`components/`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/tree/v0.3.0/components) directory
- Reference the [eth-price-oracle](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/tree/v0.3.0/components/eth-price-oracle) example:
  ```rust
  // Implement Guest trait for your component
  impl Guest for Component {
      fn run(action: TriggerAction) -> Result<Option<Vec<u8>>, String> {
          // 1. Decode trigger input
          // 2. Process data
          // 3. Return encoded result
      }
  }
  ```
- Use [`wavs_wasi_chain`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/components/eth-price-oracle/Cargo.toml) crate for chain interactions

### 3. Smart Contract Integration
- Define trigger contracts in [`src/contracts/`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/tree/v0.3.0/src/contracts)
- Implement service handler using [`IWavsServiceHandler`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/src/interfaces/IWavsSubmit.sol)
- Configure submission logic in your handler contract

### 4. Build and Deploy
```bash
# Build WASM components and Solidity contracts
make build

# Deploy service with configuration from Makefile
make deploy-service
```

## Key Interfaces

### Component Interface
[`bindings.rs`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/components/eth-price-oracle/src/bindings.rs):
```rust
trait Guest {
    // Process trigger action and return optional bytes result
    fn run(action: TriggerAction) -> Result<Option<Vec<u8>>, String>;
}

// TriggerAction contains the input data from the trigger event
struct TriggerAction {
    pub data: Vec<u8>,
}
```

### Service Handler Interface
[`IWavsServiceHandler.sol`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/src/interfaces/IWavsSubmit.sol):
```solidity
interface IWavsServiceHandler {
    /// @notice Handle signed data from WAVS operators
    /// @param _data The data to handle
    /// @param _signature The signature of the data
    function handleSignedData(bytes calldata _data, bytes calldata _signature) external;
}

interface ISimpleSubmit {
    function isValidTriggerId(TriggerId _triggerId) external view returns (bool);
    function getSignature(TriggerId _triggerId) external view returns (bytes memory);
    function getData(TriggerId _triggerId) external view returns (bytes memory);
}
```


### Quick Start
```bash
# 1. Create project
forge init --template Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template my-wavs && cd my-wavs

# 2. Setup environment
make setup && cp .env.example .env

# 3. Build everything
make build

# 4. Start local services (keep running)
make start-all

# 5. Deploy and test
TRIGGER_EVENT="NewTrigger(bytes)" make deploy-service
```

### 1. Environment Setup

#### A. System Requirements
- Docker and Docker Compose for container management
- Make for build automation
- Node.js v21+ for JavaScript dependencies
- Rust v1.84+ with wasm32-wasip2 target for WASI components
- JQ for JSON processing

#### Project Initialization
```bash
# Create new project from template
forge init --template Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template my-wavs
cd my-wavs

# Install dependencies
make setup  # Installs npm packages and git submodules

# Configure environment
cp .env.example .env  # Copy and edit for your needs
```

### 2. Development Process

#### A. Component Development
Location: [`components/`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/tree/v0.3.0/components)
```bash
# Build WASI components
make wasi-build  # Compiles Rust to WASM in ./compiled/

# Test component in isolation
COIN_MARKET_CAP_ID=1 make wasi-exec  # Tests price oracle component
```

#### B. Smart Contract Development
Location: [`src/contracts/`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/tree/v0.3.0/src/contracts)
```bash
# Build contracts
forge build

# Run contract tests
forge test
```

#### C. Full Build
```bash
make build  # Builds both components and contracts
```

### 3. Local Testing Environment

#### A. Start Infrastructure
```bash
# Start local blockchain and WAVS services
make start-all  # Keep running in separate terminal
```

#### B. Deploy Contracts
```bash
# 1. Get EigenLayer service manager
export SERVICE_MANAGER_ADDR=`make get-eigen-service-manager-from-deploy`

# 2. Deploy trigger and submission contracts
forge script ./script/Deploy.s.sol ${SERVICE_MANAGER_ADDR} \
    --sig "run(string)" \
    --rpc-url http://localhost:8545 \
    --broadcast

# 3. Note contract addresses
echo "Trigger contract: $(make get-trigger-from-deploy)"
echo "Handler contract: $(make get-service-handler-from-deploy)"
```

### 4. Service Deployment

#### A. Deploy WAVS Service
```bash
# Deploy service with trigger event configuration
TRIGGER_EVENT="NewTrigger(bytes)" make deploy-service
```

#### B. Integration Testing
```bash
# 1. Trigger service (example with Bitcoin price)
export COIN_MARKET_CAP_ID=1
export SERVICE_TRIGGER_ADDR=`make get-trigger-from-deploy`

forge script ./script/Trigger.s.sol ${SERVICE_TRIGGER_ADDR} ${COIN_MARKET_CAP_ID} \
    --sig "run(string,string)" \
    --rpc-url http://localhost:8545 \
    --broadcast -v 4

# 2. Check results
make show-result
```

### 5. Development Tools

#### A. Configuration
Key variables in [`Makefile`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/Makefile):
```makefile
# Component settings
COMPONENT_FILENAME=eth_price_oracle.wasm
TRIGGER_EVENT="NewTrigger(bytes)"
SERVICE_CONFIG='{"fuel_limit":100000000,"max_gas":5000000}'
```

#### B. Utility Commands
```bash
make help     # List all commands
make clean    # Clean build artifacts
make fmt      # Format code
```

### Development Tips

#### Best Practices
1. Always keep `make start-all` running in a separate terminal during development
2. Use `make wasi-exec` to test components in isolation before deployment
3. Check contract addresses with `make get-trigger-from-deploy` and `make get-service-handler-from-deploy`
4. Monitor service logs in the `make start-all` terminal for debugging
5. Use `make clean` when switching branches or after failed builds

#### Common Issues
1. If `make start-all` fails:
   - Check Docker is running
   - Run `make clean` and try again
   - Verify ports 8545 (Anvil) and 3000 (WAVS) are free

2. If component build fails:
   - Ensure Rust toolchain is up to date
   - Check wasm32-wasip2 target is installed
   - Verify component code follows WASI interface

3. If contract deployment fails:
   - Verify Anvil is running (`make start-all`)
   - Check contract addresses in `.env`
   - Ensure enough test ETH in deployer account

#### Debugging Tools
```bash
# View service logs
docker compose logs -f wavs

# Check component status
make get-service-status

# Verify contract deployment
make verify-contracts

# Test network connection
curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" --data '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"eth_blockNumber","params":[],"id":1}' http://localhost:8545
```

## Project Configuration

### Build Configuration
- [`foundry.toml`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/foundry.toml): Foundry settings for Solidity development
- [`Cargo.toml`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/Cargo.toml): Rust workspace and dependency configuration
- [`package.json`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/package.json): NPM dependencies and scripts

### WAVS Configuration
- [`wavs.toml`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/wavs.toml): WAVS node configuration
- [`cli.toml`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/cli.toml): CLI tool settings
- [`aggregator.toml`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/aggregator.toml): Aggregator node settings

### Docker Setup
- [`docker-compose.yml`](https://github.com/Lay3rLabs/wavs-foundry-template/blob/v0.3.0/docker-compose.yml): Container configuration for WAVS services
